summary: Small rename-only cleanup driven by `rename_symbols.py`, replacing outdated numeric `func_`/`lbl_` references with names recovered from `main.elf`. The changes touched comments, prototypes, and assembly labels/calls across MSL string routines, sysdolphin baselib matrix/vector allocation helpers, Ness yoyo assembly, and Captain/GameWatch fighter headers. No review discussion was present.
what_changed: Renamed the Ness yoyo position symbol in assembly from `lbl_803B75A4` to `YoyoChargePos` in `ftNess_AttackHi4_Release_Action`.
Updated three calls in `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s` from `func_80022C30` to the identified library function `atan2f`.
Renamed baselib allocation initializer assembly globals from `func_8037A714` to `HSD_VecInitAllocData` and from `func_8037A750` to `HSD_MtxInitAllocData`.
Changed MSL string routine comments from raw addresses/function IDs to real symbol names: `__StringRead`, `strchr`, `strncmp`, `strncpy`, and `strlen`.
Updated Captain Falcon/Ganondorf function comments and header prototypes from `func_800E2888`/`func_800E2AEC` to `ftCaptain_OnDeath` and `ftCaptain_OnLoad`.
Updated Game & Watch header prototypes from `func_8014A698`/`func_8014A6E0` to `ftGameWatch_OnItemInvisible` and `ftGameWatch_OnItemVisible`.
smart_moves: Used `rename_symbols.py` to apply known `main.elf` symbol information mechanically, reducing stale anonymous symbols without changing logic.
Kept the PR narrow: only 21 added and 21 deleted lines across six files, making it easy to verify as a rename cleanup.
Propagated renamed symbols into both assembly and C headers where relevant, improving cross-file consistency for later decompilation work.
Preserved existing function bodies and assembly instructions while only changing symbol spellings, minimizing matching risk.
decomp_lessons: When symbols are recovered from `main.elf`, update comments, headers, and assembly labels together so future matching work starts from meaningful names rather than `func_`/`lbl_` placeholders.
Rename-only PRs are useful infrastructure: they improve searchability and reviewer understanding without requiring behavioral changes.
Assembly call targets can be meaningfully named once identified, as with `func_80022C30` becoming `atan2f`; this helps infer surrounding math code intent.
Header prototypes should be kept in sync with implemented function names to avoid obsolete `func_` declarations lingering after source files are renamed.
Even comments that only document original labels matter for project navigation; updating them to real symbols improves grep-based workflows.
naming_conventions: `ftCaptain_OnLoad` and `ftCaptain_OnDeath` follow the fighter callback naming pattern `ft<Character>_<Callback>`.
`ftGameWatch_OnItemInvisible` and `ftGameWatch_OnItemVisible` follow the fighter item callback naming pattern `ft<Character>_OnItem<VisibilityState>`.
`HSD_VecInitAllocData` and `HSD_MtxInitAllocData` use the baselib/HSD prefix plus type-specific operation naming.
`YoyoChargePos` replaces an anonymous data label with a gameplay-meaningful object/state name.
Known standard-library functions should use canonical names such as `strchr`, `strncmp`, `strncpy`, `strlen`, and `atan2f` instead of address-derived placeholders.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Rename assembly `.global` labels and corresponding function labels without altering instruction bytes when symbol identity is known.
Replace branch/call target names in assembly comments/instructions, such as `bl func_80022C30` to `bl atan2f`, while preserving the same resolved address.
Use recovered symbol names from `main.elf` as a low-risk way to make un-decompiled assembly more understandable before full C matching.
For C files that already have named functions, update stale address comments so scratch links and code comments no longer advertise obsolete `func_` names.
follow_up_queries: Find other stale `func_` or `lbl_` references that `rename_symbols.py` can replace from `main.elf`.
Check whether `HSD_VecInitAllocData` and `HSD_MtxInitAllocData` have matching C declarations in baselib headers.
Search fighter headers for remaining item callback prototypes still named as `func_########`.
Audit MSL source comments for address labels that can be replaced by canonical library function names.
